video:video.video:title: US Senator Lindsey Graham has died after brief and unexpected illness, his office says
video:video.video:description: U.S. Sen. Lindsey Graham, one of President Donald Trump's closest allies in Congress who traveled the globe to advocate for a more muscular U.S. foreign policy abroad, died Saturday evening after a “brief and sudden illness,” his office said in a statement on social media.

video:video.video:title: 20 days on, Venezuelans still trying to recover bodies of their relatives killed in earthquakes
video:video.video:description: 20 days on, Venezuelans still trying to recover bodies of their relatives killed in earthquakes

video:video.video:title: Israeli strikes in Gaza kill at least five, Palestinian health officials say
video:video.video:description: Israeli strike in Gaza on Sunday killed at least five Palestinians and wounded over a dozen others, according to local health officials.

video:video.video:title: Footage from outside Bangkok music bar shows deadly fire
video:video.video:description: A huge fire tore through a music bar in Bangkok overnight into Monday, killing at least 27 people and injuring dozens.

video:video.video:title: As audiences rediscover The Odyssey, Greek classrooms keep the epic alive
video:video.video:description: As the world prepares for a Hollywood adaptation of The Odyssey, Greek students continue a tradition that has endured for centuries.

video:video.video:title: Video shows huge fire at a Bangkok beer hall that killed 27
video:video.video:description: Authorities are still investigating the cause of the fire, but Bangkok Gov. Chadchart Sittipunt said investigators would examine the ceiling materials and whether any emergency exits may have been obstructed, potentially making it more difficult for people to evacuate.

video:video.video:title: US Senator Lindsey Graham died after aorta tear, medical examiner says
video:video.video:description: The tear in the inner wall of the aorta, called an aortic dissection, was related to the hardening of Graham's arteries.

video:video.video:title: Coalition of the Willing meeting joined by Zelenskyy opens in Paris
video:video.video:description: “Our goal is to build a shared ballistic missile defense capability for Europe,” the 10 nations said in a statement in Paris at talks with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.

video:video.video:title: US military uses drone boats in a first-of-its-kind attack
video:video.video:description: U.S. Central Command says it used drone ships to hit an Iranian ship maintenance facility and submarine, calling it first.

video:video.video:title: Tom Holland delayed 'Spider-Man' to be in 'The Odyssey'
video:video.video:description: Tom Holland says he was "nervous" asking Sony to push production for "Spider-Man: Brand New Day" so he could star in "The Odyssey."

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 13
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday, July 13th: U.S. launches new strikes in Iran; U.S. and Iran claim control of Strait of Hormuz; Bangkok beer hall fire kills dozens; Aorta tear blamed for Senator Lindsey Graham’s death.

video:video.video:title: US military says its latest wave of strikes targeting Iran is over and hit dozens of targets
video:video.video:description: US military says its latest wave of strikes targeting Iran is over, insisting Tehran does not control Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: New Zealand actor Sam Neill, known for 'Jurassic Park' and 'The Piano,' dies at 78
video:video.video:description: Actor Sam Neill's death was “sudden and unexpected,” a family statement said, adding that he “remained cancer free” when he died. A cause of death wasn’t specified.

video:video.video:title: US Senator Lindsey Graham, a close Trump ally, dies after brief and unexpected illness
video:video.video:description: Sen. Lindsey Graham, one of President Donald Trump's closest allies in Congress who traveled the globe to advocate for a more aggressive U.S. foreign policy, has died after a “brief and sudden illness,” his office said.

video:video.video:title: Fire breaks out at a beer hall in Bangkok, killing at least 27 people
video:video.video:description: A huge fire tore through a beer hall in Bangkok overnight into Monday, killing at least 27 people and injuring dozens before firefighters brought the blaze under control.

video:video.video:title: Man who killed Dartmouth professors at 17 to get a chance at parole in about 20 years
video:video.video:description: A Vermont man who was 17 when he and a friend killed a pair of married Dartmouth College professors 25 years ago will have a chance at parole in about 20 years, when he reaches the age of one of his victims, a judge ruled Monday.

video:video.video:title: Moscow to 'respond in kind' to Ukraine with more powerful strikes, Putin says
video:video.video:description: President Vladimir Putin on Monday said Moscow's response to Ukrainian attacks would always be "mirror-like" but "several times more powerful", he said as he toured an exhibition organised by the pro-Kremlin coalition All-Russia People's Front.

video:video.video:title: Chinese and Russian ships join naval drills in Yellow Sea
video:video.video:description: According to Russia’s Defense Ministry, ships from the two countries spent several days conducting joint drills, including live-fire exercises using artillery and machine-gun systems to repel a simulated attack by unmanned aerial vehicles and unmanned surface vessels.

video:video.video:title: AP Explains: Senate returns to Washington after Sen. Lindsey Graham's death with uncertain agenda
video:video.video:description: Senate Republicans will return to Washington on Monday with an uncertain agenda after the sudden death of prominent Republican Lindsey Graham, a committee chairman and key player who served as a crucial ally with President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: Iran says initial deal with US in state of 'crisis' and blames Washington
video:video.video:description: Iran said on Monday that an initial peace deal reached with the United States last month is in a state of "crisis" as Iran's military released footage it said showed missiles being launched earlier in the day in response to what it described as "US aggressions".

video:video.video:title: Oil prices rise following the latest fighting in the Middle East, as AI stocks sink
video:video.video:description: Oil prices jumped Monday following a weekend of attacks in the Middle East, while more losses for computer chip companies and other winners of the artificial-intelligence boom weighed on stock markets.

video:video.video:title: Massive AI buildout drives up costs for laptops and electricity
video:video.video:description: American consumers are being hit with another high-cost headache. The surge of investment in data centers to power artificial intelligence has made memory chips, computer processors and other equipment, as well as electricity, more expensive.

video:video.video:title: Pitbull fans break bald cap world record
video:video.video:description: Pitbull fans broke the Guinness World Record for the largest gathering of people wearing bald caps. AP Entertainment Journalist Louise Dixon joined in on the fun.

video:video.video:title: Argentina fans in Buenos Aires jubilant as team beats Switzerland to reach World Cup semifinals
video:video.video:description: Jubilant Argentinian fans in Buenos Aires celebrated into the early hours of the morning after their country beat Switzerland in the World Cup to advance to the semifinals of the tournament.

video:video.video:title: Hundreds of firefighters backed by aircraft continue to battle one of Spain's deadliest wildfires
video:video.video:description: Residents in Spain's Almeria province described the moment they fled one of the country's deadliest wildfires this week.

video:video.video:title: England fans in London react after team beats Norway to reach World Cup semi-finals
video:video.video:description: Soccer fans in London celebrated England's World Cup victory over Norway on Saturday and reacted in high spirits. (AP video shot by Kwiyeon Ha)

video:video.video:title: Prince Harry's 'goated' moment at UK summer festival
video:video.video:description: Prince Harry tried out goat yoga as he attended an English summer festival on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: England fans in Florida celebrate victory over Norway in World Cup quarter-finals
video:video.video:description: English fans burst into celebration after England's victory over Norway in a World Cup quarter final match on Saturday. (AP Video by Sophie Bates)

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 12
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, July 12: Sen. Lindsey Graham dies at 71; U.S.-Iran tensions rise after Strait of Hormuz attacks; Vietnam detains captain after deadly boat crash; McGregor’s UFC return ends with knee injury.

video:video.video:title: US flag lowered to half-staff at White House after death of Lindsey Graham
video:video.video:description: U.S. flags at the White House, and its adjacent buildings, were lowered to half-staff Sunday following the death of Sen. Lindsey Graham.

video:video.video:title: Philadelphia area digs out after a series of severe microburst storms
video:video.video:description: The storms came through Saturday downing trees and power lines, flooding several streets, and even causing at least one building to collapse, throwing bricks and debris into the street, crushing cars and blocking trolley tracks.

video:video.video:title: Storm surge slams into windows as Typhoon Bavi hits China's Taizhou city
video:video.video:description: A storm surge slammed against windows as Typhoon Bavi barreled into China's eastern coast, bringing heavy rain and huge waves to Zhejiang province late Saturday.

video:video.video:title: South Carolina residents reflect following the death of Sen. Lindsey Graham
video:video.video:description: Residents of Fort Mill, South Carolina, react to the death of Sen. Lindsey Graham, who served in Congress for more than three decades, had turned 71 years old just two days before dying on Saturday night.

video:video.video:title: Aftermath of deadly fire that broke out at pub in Bangkok
video:video.video:description: At least 27 people died in a huge fire that had engulfed a pub in Bangkok early on Monday morning, Thailand's Prime Minister told the reporters at the scene.

video:video.video:title: World Cup stickers traded en masse in Mexico City as part of world record attempt
video:video.video:description: Thousands of people gathered Sunday in Mexico City's Zócalo to swap 2026 World Cup soccer stickers as part of a Guinness World Record attempt.

video:video.video:title: China’s 'Green Great Wall' tames desert growth, but scientists warn the fight is not over
video:video.video:description: For half a century, workers in northern China have been using a technique called "straw checkerboards" to combat desertification.

video:video.video:title: Hundreds of firefighters battle wildfire in Almeria, southern Spain
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of firefighters backed by helicopters and fixed-wing aircraft struggled to contain one of Spain's deadliest wildfires that erupted earlier this week.

video:video.video:title: Qatar’s Emir, royal family and senior officials attend funeral of late former Emir in Doha
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of mourners gathered at the Imam Muhammad bin Abdul Wahhab Mosque to pay tribute to and bid farewell to late former Emir of Qatar Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani.

video:video.video:title: As audiences rediscover The Odyssey, Greek classrooms keep the epic alive
video:video.video:description: As the world prepares for a Hollywood adaptation of The Odyssey, Greek students continue a tradition that has endured for centuries.

video:video.video:title: Syria’s new parliament holds first session since Assad’s fall and elects its speaker
video:video.video:description: Syria's newly elected parliament held its first session since the ouster of former President Bashar Assad on Sunday, hoping to restart the legislative process in the country after years of conflict and autocratic rule.

video:video.video:title: Palestinian in Gaza dedicates himself to restoring burnt or torn copies of the Quran
video:video.video:description: In a refugee camp on the northern Mediterranean shores of the Gaza Strip, Jameel Miqdad sat behind stacks of damaged copies of the Quran.

video:video.video:title: Japan space agency's reusable rocket lands safely in first test flight
video:video.video:description: The RV-X rocket lifted off, hovered and moved horizontally before landing during its less than one-minute flight at the Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency’s (JAXA) Noshiro Testing Center in northeastern Japan.

video:video.video:title: What you need to know about Strait of Hormuz as Iran and US reach a tentative deal to open waterway
video:video.video:description: The United States and Iran reached an initial agreement early Monday to open the Strait of Hormuz and further extend a shaky ceasefire in the Iran war, potentially allowing desperately needed oil and natural gas to reach the global market.

video:video.video:title: Zendaya reveals who told her she'd play playing Athena
video:video.video:description: Zendaya just revealed the surprising way she found out she'd landed the role of Athena in Christopher Nolan's "The Odyssey"

video:video.video:title: CCTV captures civilians taking cover during Russian strike on Sumy in Ukraine
video:video.video:description: CCTV footage from the Ukrainian city of Sumy captured the moment visitors at a local coffee shop scrambled for cover during a Russian air strike.

video:video.video:title: Fire in Bangkok pub kills at least 27 people
video:video.video:description: A fire which engulfed a beer hall in Bangkok overnight killed at least 27 people and injured dozens more. Firefighters brought the blaze under control early Monday.

video:video.video:title: South Carolina Sen. Lindsey Graham dies at 71
video:video.video:description: Sen. Lindsey Graham, one of President Donald Trump’s closest allies in Congress who traveled the globe to advocate for a more aggressive U.S. foreign policy, died after a tear in his aorta, according to a preliminary medical examiner finding shared by his office.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 13
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, July 13th: Hospital workers in Ebola-stricken Congo strike over wages; Zelenskyy awards Macron the Order of Freedom; The UN condemns continued military confrontation between Iran and the US; Spanish researchers discover sugar in space.

video:video.video:title: Pomp and tradition meets conservation in annual swan count on the river Thames
video:video.video:description: The annual count of swans belonging to King Charles III has begun on the river Thames. The five day event is known as swan upping and dates back to the 12th Century.

video:video.video:title: Witness heard man say “I tried to stop” after ICE shooting in Maine
video:video.video:description: A federal immigration officer fatally shot a motorist in Maine on Monday, the second time in a week that U.S.

video:video.video:title: Racist comments targeting France’s World Cup team spark backlash
video:video.video:description: Comments questioning the nationality of France’s players have drawn backlash from French officials and others, reigniting a debate over racism and representation in soccer.

video:video.video:title: Feds hand over evidence in Renee Good and Alex Pretti killings to Minnesota prosecutors
video:video.video:description: Minnesota prosecutors announced Monday that they have obtained key evidence in their ongoing investigations into fatal shootings of Renee Good and Alex Pretti during pitched protests against a federal immigration enforcement crackdown in the state earlier this year.

video:video.video:title: California leads 12-state bid to freeze Paramount-Warner Bros. Discovery deal
video:video.video:description: Twelve states challenged Paramount’s takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery on Monday, filing a lawsuit that argues the mega merger would “extinguish competition” in Hollywood and threaten jobs across the industry.

video:video.video:title: Darline Graham Nordone, sister of Lindsey Graham, picked to serve remainder of US Senate term
video:video.video:description: Lindsey Graham’s sister, Darline Graham Nordone, has been named as her late brother's temporary replacement in the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Senate returns to Washington after Sen. Lindsey Graham's death with uncertain agenda, AP Explains
video:video.video:description: Senate Republicans will return to Washington on Monday with an uncertain agenda after the sudden death of prominent Republican Lindsey Graham, a committee chairman and key player who served as a crucial ally with President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: 'The Odyssey': A character guide in 60 seconds
video:video.video:description: Christopher Nolan's "The Odyssey" premieres July 17. Here's a quick guide to the main characters from the nearly 3,000-year-old epic before you head to the theater.

video:video.video:title: Lindsey Graham’s sister, Darline Graham Nordone, will serve out remainder of his Senate term
video:video.video:description: Lindsey Graham’s sister, Darline Graham Nordone, has been named as her late brother's temporary replacement in the U.S. Senate.

video:video.video:title: Multiple blasts as Sanaa airport hit in airstrikes
video:video.video:description: Multiple blasts were seen at Sanaa airport in Yemen on Monday in what Iran-backed Houthi rebels said were Saudi airstrikes while the internationally recognized government in Yemen said they were meant to prevent an Iranian plane from landing.

video:video.video:title: Senators deliver emotional tributes to Lindsey Graham after his sudden death
video:video.video:description: Sen. Lindsey Graham’s desk was covered in black cloth and a vase of white roses as the Senate opened Monday afternoon.

video:video.video:title: Protesters hold walking vigil after ICE fatally shoots motorist in Maine
video:video.video:description: A federal immigration officer fatally shot a motorist in Maine on Monday. Dozens of demonstrators protesting ICE and President Donald Trump's immigration crackdown held a walking vigil in Biddeford that evening.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Iran war moving ‘very fast,’ US will seek control of Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Monday the war with Iran is moving “very fast” as U.S. forces pressed ahead with strikes targeting Tehran’s military capabilities, adding that Washington would seek to control the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: In a sweet discovery, astronomers find sugar lurking in the space between stars
video:video.video:description: The space between stars in the Milky Way just got a little sweeter for Spanish researchers who've found evidence of a sugar which is found in raspberries and self tanning lotions.

video:video.video:title: Morning shots from scene of Bankgkok pub fire that killed at least 27
video:video.video:description: Most of the people who were killed in a huge fire in a Bangkok music bar were found trapped in windowless bathrooms where they may have sought to escape the flames that claimed at least 27 lives, authorities said Monday as investigations began.

video:video.video:title: Workers at Ebola center in Congo strike over unpaid salaries
video:video.video:description: Dozens of people working at an Ebola treatment center in the Rwampara general hospital in northeast Democratic Republic of Congo went on strike Monday over unpaid salaries and bonuses, shutting down the hospital, blocking the road leading to the medical facility and burning tires in front of the mai

video:video.video:title: Firefighters deployed across Paris region forest to fight exceptional fire
video:video.video:description: It was among several wildfires in western Europe as the region bakes under its third red-alert heat wave this year.

video:video.video:title: Spanish PM flies over scene of deadly wildfire in Almeria
video:video.video:description: Spain is experiencing extreme heat, which combined with wind and little rainfall is creating the ideal conditions for small wildfires to grow unchecked.

video:video.video:title: Grandmothers in Kenya ask for a soccer World Cup to compete in
video:video.video:description: As the football world’s attention is caught by the World Cup, there is a group of unlikely fans from rural Kenya who want to prove that the elegant sport belongs to all generations.

video:video.video:title: US attacks Iran as Tehran retaliates against UAE tankers in Strait of Hormuz and Bahrain
video:video.video:description: The U.S. launched strikes on Iran early Tuesday morning, hours after President Donald Trump said Washington is “reinstating” a blockade on Iran in the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Kerry Washington on reviving 'The Whoopi Monologues'
video:video.video:description: Kerry Washington stars in and produces a revival of Whoopi Goldberg's 1984 Broadway show "The Whoopi Monologues.” (July 13)

video:video.video:title: Reese Witherspoon reflects on 25th anniversary of 'Legally Blonde'
video:video.video:description: On the 25th anniversary of "Legally Blonde," Reese Witherspoon says playing "ultimate underdog" Elle Woods "changed my life."

video:video.video:title: Whoopi Goldberg reflects on 'The Whoopi Monologues'
video:video.video:description: As "The Whoopi Monologues" gets an Off-Broadway revival featuring Kerry Washington, Whoopi Goldberg looks back at her inspirations. (July 13)

video:video.video:title: Anya Taylor-Joy's World Cup 'stress'
video:video.video:description: Anya Taylor-Joy says the World Cup semifinal between England and Argentina has brought "a lot of stress" to her household because of her ties to both countries.
